name: deEHR
description: Open-source patient-owned Electronic Health Record platform built on FHIR R4 and SMART App Launch standards, with selective anchoring on the Klever blockchain (no PHI on-chain). Targets LGPD, HIPAA, and integration with the Brazilian RNDS at the boundary.
description_good_justification: Repo description and README state purpose: open-source patient-owned EHR on FHIR R4 + SMART standards with Klever blockchain anchoring (no PHI on-chain).
interact_justification: Public GitHub Issues and PR discussions on https://github.com/brunocampos-ssa/deEHR.
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md covers Conventional Commits, signed commits, branch naming, PR process, mandatory security audit before each PR, and the hard invariants (no PHI on-chain, no real data in repo).
floss_license_justification: Licensed under the MIT License — released as FLOSS.
documentation_basics_justification: README.md provides project overview, architecture, status, and phase plan. CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md document workflow and reporting.
documentation_interface_justification: Phase 0 (Foundations) — no external API exposed yet. Will apply in Phase 1 when the FHIR gateway and SMART auth server ship.
sites_https_justification: Project is hosted on github.com which serves all pages over HTTPS with HSTS.
discussion_justification: Public discussion via GitHub Issues and PR comment threads.
english_justification: Canonical documentation is in English. Brazilian-Portuguese (pt-BR) mirrors are maintained alongside per documentation policy in CONTRIBUTING.md.
maintained_justification: Actively maintained — repository created 2026-05-22; six PRs merged in the past week. Solo maintainer (brunocampos-ssa) responds to issues within days.
repo_track_justification: Git is used for version control.
repo_interim_justification: All intermediate commits are visible in Git history. Trunk-based development; squash or rebase merges per ADR-0003.
repo_distributed_justification: Git is a distributed version control system.
version_unique_justification: No releases yet (Phase 0). Per CONTRIBUTING.md and ADR-0003 each future release will have a unique signed git tag (vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH).
version_semver_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md and ADR-0003 specify Semantic Versioning.
version_tags_justification: Releases will be cut as signed git tags per ADR-0003. No tags yet because Phase 0 has not shipped functionality.
release_notes_justification: No releases yet — project is in Phase 0 (Foundations). Release notes policy is documented in ADR-0003 and will be in place before the first tagged release.
release_notes_vulns_justification: No releases yet and no known vulnerabilities.
report_process_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents the bug-report process (GitHub Issues with issue templates). SECURITY.md documents the vulnerability disclosure process.
report_tracker_justification: Public bug tracker via GitHub Issues with structured templates.
report_responses_justification: Solo maintainer actively triages all incoming issues. All Phase 0 issues raised so far have been addressed or are tracked with an owner.
enhancement_responses_justification: Enhancement proposals are tracked as GitHub Issues and Architecture Decision Records (docs/architecture/). Examples: ADR-0001 through ADR-0005 — each opened, discussed, and Accepted or Proposed within days.
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues and PR threads provide a public, archived record of reports, discussion, and resolution.
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: SECURITY.md describes the responsible-disclosure process and the contact method.
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting is enabled (Security tab → Report a vulnerability).
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: SECURITY.md commits to acknowledging vulnerability reports.
build_justification: Phase 0 (Foundations) — no compiled artifacts produced yet. Will apply once Go services and Rust/WASM contracts ship in Phase 1.
build_common_tools_justification: No build process yet (Phase 0). When introduced, Go services will build with the standard Go toolchain and Rust contracts with cargo + wasm32 target — both common FLOSS tools.
build_floss_tools_justification: No build process yet (Phase 0). Future build will rely only on FLOSS toolchains (Go, cargo, rustc).
test_justification: Phase 0 (Foundations) — no test suite yet because there is no production code yet. CONTRIBUTING.md mandates tests for any behaviour change once code lands (see test_policy).
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: No test suite yet (Phase 0). CI is already configured for documentation/security: .github/workflows/lint.yml (markdownlint, actionlint) and security.yml (gitleaks, Trivy fs, OpenSSF Scorecard, CodeQL). Test CI will be added when production code lands.
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md states: 'Add or update tests for any behaviour change.' Tests are required for code PRs.
warnings_justification: No compiled code yet (Phase 0). Rust contract crate skeletons pass `cargo check` cleanly with no warnings. Strict warnings will be enforced (rustc, clippy, go vet) when production code lands.
warnings_strict_justification: No compiled code yet (Phase 0). Project policy in CONTRIBUTING.md is strict warnings: gofmt, go vet, rustfmt, clippy with no lints.
know_secure_design_justification: STRIDE threat model per area at docs/security/threat-model.md. Architecture Decision Records (ADR-0001 to ADR-0005) explicitly discuss security trade-offs. Hard invariants (no PHI on-chain, no PHI in repo, security as a gate not a phase) documented in CONTRIBUTING.md.
know_common_errors_justification: Threat model and ADRs address common error classes per area (Identity Service, FHIR gateway, smart contracts, RNDS connector). CONTRIBUTING.md flags overflow, reentrancy, and WASM-specific issues for Rust contracts.
crypto_published_justification: Phase 0 — no shipped cryptographic code yet. ADR-0004 specifies Ed25519 (klv-1) for authentication/capabilityInvocation and post-quantum schemes via multicodec for assertionMethod/keyAgreement; all algorithms are published and well-known.
crypto_call_justification: Phase 0 — no shipped code that calls crypto yet. Design (ADR-0004) calls only KVM-native verifiers (verify_ed25519, sha256) and crate-level Rust implementations of established algorithms.
crypto_keylength_justification: Phase 0 — no shipped code yet. ADR-0004 specifies Ed25519 (~128-bit security) plus a multi-codec PQ scheme for assertion/keyAgreement.
crypto_working_justification: Phase 0 — no shipped code yet. Specified algorithms (Ed25519, SHA-256, NIST PQ candidates) have no publicly known practical breaks.
crypto_weaknesses_justification: Phase 0 — no shipped code yet. Specification excludes broken primitives (no MD5, no SHA-1, no DES, no RC4).
crypto_pfs_justification: Phase 0 — no key-agreement code shipped yet. Transport security relies on TLS (GitHub today; production gateway in Phase 1 will use TLS 1.3 with PFS-only suites).
crypto_password_storage_justification: Identity design (ADR-0001, ADR-0004) is passkey + DID-based. Passwords are not part of the authentication model — no password storage.
crypto_random_justification: Phase 0 — no shipped code yet. Specification requires CSPRNG (OS-provided) for key generation and nonces.
delivery_mitm_justification: Source delivered via GitHub over HTTPS / SSH; commits to main are GPG-signed (enforced by branch ruleset).
delivery_unsigned_justification: No package downloads yet (no releases). Commits to main must be GPG-signed (enforced by branch ruleset). Future release artefacts will be signed.
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: No known vulnerabilities. No shipped functionality yet (Phase 0).
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: No known vulnerabilities. Private vulnerability reporting is enabled for fast disclosure.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: Gitleaks runs as a required status check on every PR (.github/workflows/security.yml). CONTRIBUTING.md prohibits committing real data, credentials, secrets, or tokens.
static_analysis_justification: No code yet (Phase 0). CodeQL is already wired into the main-branch ruleset as a required status check and will engage automatically when Go services and Rust contracts ship in Phase 1.
static_analysis_often_justification: No code yet (Phase 0). CodeQL will run on every PR once code lands.
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: Design uses only memory-safe languages (Go, Rust). No C/C++ planned anywhere in the stack.
